title: reCaptcha for monday.com forms
excerpt:
Hi! 
Is there a way to add recaptcha for monday forms? 
We created form which we will be embed on our website and it would be great to secure forms with e.g. recaptcha to avoid spam :smiley:

posts.author: the.rocket (Konrad Pawlak)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!

Is there a way to add recaptcha for monday forms?
We created form which we will be embed on our website and it would be great to secure forms with e.g. recaptcha to avoid spam

posts.author: Francoise (Francoise)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, I do not see how to use the embed form without a ReCaptcha…any popular websites will have tons of spam

posts.author: erfan (erfan)
posts.cookedHtml: Any progress or update on this? Having a public form is not possible now because of spam.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
Having this capability in standard monday forms would be great.

Until this happens, one options is Jotform. It allows multiple CAPTCHA types and integrates easily with monday.

Jim - Subscribe to The Monday Man
Watch Our Latest Video: Quick Tip - The Trick to Editing Custom Automations

posts.author: jgilbert (Joyce Gilbert)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m trying to get this back to the top of the requested list. The forms in Monday are very limited in use if we can’t have a way to secure them from spam.

Hoping this will get in the works soon. Thanks!

posts.author: betapter (Peter Stelzhammer)
posts.cookedHtml: This is necessary, please add. No form on a website without reCAPTCHA, thanks!

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT this appears to be released in workforms.

image318×746 25.1 KB

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Correct! Thank you for letting me know

posts.author: kevin_vaghasiya (kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
For anyone still dealing with spam on embedded forms, FormFlow gives you both Google reCAPTCHA and Cloudflare Turnstile as options. You can also customise the error messages and stack it with password protection and submission rules.

We had bots hammering one of our website forms and the combination of Turnstile + submission limits basically killed the spam overnight.
